highlights.text: Targets specified on Thursday at 4pm were: “My lean is bulls can try a final bounce here with 6694/6702 first up, 6715 2nd, then 6731 above.” We ripped to 6715 3pm Friday.
My lean is we work up it to 6732, 6744, then 6766/83.” This could not worked out better and right off the open Sunday we began the trek upwards, hitting 6783 by this afternoon to set the high of day.

These building blocks are: Elevator down sells (this refers to when ES knifes through every support), Failed Breakdowns (my core setup, this follows elevator down), then Back-Tests (the magnets for these short squeezes).
Early Friday morning, ES then collapsed and went elevator down to 6573.
I wrote on Thursday at 4pm: “Obvious setup here the recovery of Tuesday’s 6593 daily low.” We held 6572, recovered 6593 early Friday morning, then ripped all day.
This was a classic Failed Breakdown, we see them every day nearly, and this was a great example to learn from.
I wrote on Tuesday at 4pm that: “6766 is a lower risk spot to short”.

post_title: After a 200 Point Parabolic Squeeze Since Friday, Do SPX Bulls Have The Energy For All Time Highs? Or Sell 1st? Oct 21st Plan
